paper_metadata.title: DeBERTa: Decoding-enhanced BERT with Disentangled Attention
paper_metadata.abstract: Recent progress in pre-trained neural language models has significantly improved the performance of many natural language processing (NLP) tasks. In this paper we propose a new model architecture DeBERTa (Decoding-enhanced BERT with disentangled attention) that improves the BERT and RoBERTa models using two novel techniques. The first is the disentangled attention mechanism, where each word is represented using two vectors that encode its content and position, respectively, and the attention weights among words are computed using disentangled matrices on their contents and relative positions, respectively. Second, an enhanced mask decoder is used to incorporate absolute positions in the decoding layer to predict the masked tokens in model pre-training. In addition, a new virtual adversarial training method is used for fine-tuning to improve models' generalization. We show that these techniques significantly improve the efficiency of model pre-training and the performance of both natural language understanding (NLU) and natural langauge generation (NLG) downstream tasks. Compared to RoBERTa-Large, a DeBERTa model trained on half of the training data performs consistently better on a wide range of NLP tasks, achieving improvements on MNLI by +0.9% (90.2% vs. 91.1%), on SQuAD v2.0 by +2.3% (88.4% vs. 90.7%) and RACE by +3.6% (83.2% vs. 86.8%). Notably, we scale up DeBERTa by training a larger version that consists of 48 Transform layers with 1.5 billion parameters. The significant performance boost makes the single DeBERTa model surpass the human performance on the SuperGLUE benchmark (Wang et al., 2019a) for the first time in terms of macro-average score (89.9 versus 89.8), and the ensemble DeBERTa model sits atop the SuperGLUE leaderboard as of January 6, 2021, out performing the human baseline by a decent margin (90.3 versus 89.8).

extraction_data.innovation: DeBERTa introduces a disentangled attention mechanism, where each word is represented using two separate vectors encoding its content and position, respectively. It also incorporates an enhanced mask decoder that uses absolute position embeddings right before the softmax layer to predict masked tokens, improving syntactic nuance understanding.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Not explicitly stated (assumed large-scale text corpus)
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding (e.g., MNLI, SQuADv2.0, RACE), natural language generation (e.g., Wikitext-103), general NLP tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving pre-trained language models for natural language understanding (NLU) and natural language generation (NLG) tasks
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on downstream NLP tasks, Virtual Adversarial Training (VAT)
extraction_data.extension: Disentangled attention mechanism using separate content and position vectors, and enhanced mask decoder incorporating absolute position embeddings for masked token prediction.
extraction_data.paper_title: DeBERTa: Decoding-enhanced BERT with Disentangled Attention

extraction_data.innovation: Scaled-up version of DeBERTa with 48 Transformer layers and 1.5 billion parameters, achieving state-of-the-art performance on the SuperGLUE benchmark and surpassing human baseline for the first time.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Not explicitly stated (assumed large-scale text corpus)
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding (e.g., SuperGLUE), general NLP tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Scaling pre-trained language models for improved performance on complex NLP benchmarks
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on downstream NLP tasks, Virtual Adversarial Training (VAT)
extraction_data.extension: Disentangled attention mechanism using separate content and position vectors, and enhanced mask decoder incorporating absolute position embeddings for masked token prediction.
extraction_data.paper_title: DeBERTa: Decoding-enhanced BERT with Disentangled Attention

extraction_data.innovation: DeBERTa introduces disentangled attention, which represents each token using two separate vectors for content and relative position, enabling more nuanced attention weight calculation. It also features an Enhanced Mask Decoder (EMD) that incorporates absolute word positions right before the softmax layer for masked token prediction, improving syntactic nuance understanding.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Wikipedia, BookCorpus, OPENWEBTEXT, STORIES (total 78GB after deduplication)
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Scale-invariant Fine-Tuning (SiFT) for downstream NLP tasks
extraction_data.extension: Disentangled attention mechanism decomposes attention weights into content-to-content, content-to-position, position-to-content, and position-to-position components, and uses relative position embeddings with efficient implementation to reduce space complexity. Enhanced Mask Decoder (EMD) adds absolute position information post-Transformer layers for improved masked token prediction.
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving language understanding by enhancing attention mechanisms and masked token prediction in pre-trained language models
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, text classification, question answering, and other language tasks requiring syntactic and semantic comprehension
extraction_data.paper_title: DeBERTa: Decoding-enhanced BERT with Disentangled Attention

extraction_data.innovation: DeBERTa introduces a disentangled attention mechanism, where each word is represented using two vectors encoding its content and position, respectively. It also incorporates an enhanced mask decoder that uses absolute positions in the decoding layer to predict masked tokens during pre-training.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, question answering, natural language inference, word sense disambiguation, reasoning
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving language understanding and model pre-training efficiency
extraction_data.extension: Disentangled attention matrices for content and relative positions, and a convolution layer added as a side to the first Transformer layer to induce n-gram knowledge of sub-word encodings.
extraction_data.paper_title: DeBERTa: Decoding-enhanced BERT with Disentangled Attention

extraction_data.innovation: DeBERTa RTDbase uses Replaced Token Detection (RTD) as a more efficient pre-training task compared to Masked Language Modeling (MLM), enabling better parameter efficiency and performance.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on downstream tasks (e.g., MNLI, SQuADv2)
extraction_data.extension: Replaced Token Detection (RTD) pre-training objective replaces MLM, with a generator-discriminator framework for improved efficiency.
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving parameter efficiency and performance of pre-trained language models
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, text classification, question answering, and language modeling
extraction_data.paper_title: DeBERTa: Decoding-enhanced BERT with Disentangled Attention
